title: Resistance of calcium hydroxide materials to phosphoric acid
abstract: Abstract— Two single and nine dual product calcium hydroxide materials were tested for their resistance to penetration by a typical dental enamel etchant, phosphoric acid, 50 per cent mass/mass. The tests used a specially made, all‐glass cell incorporating a standard specimen recess and a small constant head of phosphoric acid. The period taken for the acid to traverse the specimen and strongly decrease the pH value of water in contact with the opposite surface comprised the basis for assessing relative acid resistance. The results for the various materials showed substantial variation and reflect their chemical composition.
